Scenario.title: Leviathan Australian Intrusions Healthcare Sector Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex A sophisticated threat actor group, Leviathan, has been observed targeting the healthcare sector in Australia. Their attacks involve multiple phases, including reconnaissance, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Device theft, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Vulnerability, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Autonomous system numbers(ASNs), SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Malicious insiders, Social engineering, Insufficient physical security controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: External/Removable media, Loss or Theft of equipment, Attrition, Web, Account takeover, Inadequate encryption, Browser-based attack, Application compromise, Open port, Vulnerability exploit, Ransomware, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, SIM Swap, Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities, Web forms, IP address and IP blocks, Domains and subdomains, Botnet, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Phishing, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the healthcare organization's network infrastructure and potential vulnerabilities by searching online for public information.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers engage in scanning activities to map network nodes, hosts, devices, and routes to identify potential entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers create custom tools to exploit identified vulnerabilities or weaknesses in the healthcare organization's systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers utilize publicly known exploits for healthcare software or systems to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromise employee devices through drive-by downloads, exploiting vulnerabilities in the healthcare organization's web applications.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control channels for further operations, using compromised systems to execute malicious code.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers use PowerShell for lateral movement and privilege escalation, executing malicious scripts to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain persistence and ensure continued access to the healthcare organization's systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers elevate privileges using existing elevation control mechanisms, gaining unauthorized access to sensitive areas of the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers disable or tamper with antivirus software to evade detection, ensuring their malicious activities remain undetected.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to brute-force login credentials for sensitive areas of the network, compromising authentication mechanisms.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map the victim's network and identify potential targets by engaging in active OS fingerprinting.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers move laterally within the network using remote services, exploiting vulnerabilities in the healthcare organization's systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems, extracting valuable information for future exploitation.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate stolen data via FTP, transferring sensitive information to their command and control servers.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt critical systems to cause maximum impact, disabling network hardware to prevent essential services from functioning.

Scenario.summary: The Leviathan Australian Intrusions threat actor group employs a complex attack strategy involving multiple phases, from reconnaissance to impact. They leverage various techniques, including exploit public-facing applications, drive-by compromise, command and control, and denial of service, to compromise the healthcare organization's systems and exfiltrate sensitive data.
